title: Prey

notes:
Test Configuration:

-Graphic preset: Ultra
-Resolution: 1920x1080
-Fullscreen: Enabled
-Vsync: Off

Problems:
- 2x Slower loading time compared to Windows 10
- 2x Slower texture and shadows loading time compared to Windows 10

Fps:
 ~140Fps in loaded Area
 ~15Fps with a lot of freezing in new Area(due to Texture and Shadows slow loading and rendering)

Input:
- Mouse input lag: none
- Keyboard input lag: none

Sound:
- Razer Kraken Chroma 7.1: equivalent to Windows 10

Resource:
- CPU usage: ~60% (Windows 10: ~80 to 90%)
- Ram usage: ~6Gb (Windows 10: ~7Gb)

title: Prey

notes: Game runs almost perfectly after raising the file descriptor limit in my systemd configuration with DefaultLimitNOFILE=1024:1048576. I have had a couple freezes where I have had to manually kill the process, usually when there's a phantom on screen and I'm attacking it, though that may be a coincidence. Only one crash. Performance degrades when holding turrets. And when using my Steam Controller, the game fails to load the button and action icons for the controller. All dialogs display a rainbow-static icon with the word IMG on top, instead of the proper button icons. All in all though, it's playable besides the occasional freeze once every hour or so.

title: Prey

notes:
Using Void Linux - initially it ran perfectly but it started to crash when writing  saves. After running Steam from a terminal I saw I was getting the errors:

"eventfd: Too many open files", "Protocol error: process 0025: sendmsg: Bad file descriptor", "esync: write: Bad file descriptor".


The fix was to increase the file limit by adding the line:

myusername               hard         nofile             204800

to /etc/security/limits.conf

Now that I think about it, it seems like maybe disable esync would have the same effect, didn't think to try launch options at the time though :)
title: Prey

notes:
When launching a dialog appears saying Unsupported CPU detected. CPU needs to support SSE, SSE2 and SSE3. Which it definitely does. Then when I press OK a black full screen window appears and nothing else happens.

The problem occurs on both Proton 3.16-4 and 3.7-8.

title: Prey

notes: Run  perfectly stable  at 60 fps on 1920x1080 resolution with  texture , object detail , shadow quality to medium and Anti-Aliasing  and  Anisotropic filtering on the hightest setting  and screen space  reflection , screen space direction occlusion completely off. title: Prey

notes:
I take back borked... After adding libgnutls26 from the AUR it works well (guess this will also be applicable to NieR...). Only bad things of note so far... Had to disable all screens apart from the one I'm going to play the game on (may be ok after the first run; still some verification to do), and the FoV defaulted at a whole 33 - so would recommend checking that.

Performance wise, forgot I'm actually playing on Linux.
